- Action items: specific, assigned, time-bound improvements (e.g., 'Add integration test for payment flow by March 15 - assigned to team X')
- Lessons learned: what worked well in the response, what could be improved
- Follow-up: schedule for reviewing action item completion

rubric.key: Correct definition of blameless postmortem
Explains why blame-free analysis produces better outcomes
Complete postmortem document structure

major_assignments.title: Test Strategy and Multi-Level Test Suite
major_assignments.description: Design a comprehensive test strategy for a provided web application, then implement it: write unit tests with TDD, integration tests with test containers, and E2E tests with Playwright. Achieve meaningful coverage targets based on risk analysis. Deliver a test strategy document, the automated test suite, and a quality assessment report.
objectives: Design a risk-based test strategy with justified coverage targets for each component
Implement automated tests at unit, integration, and E2E levels using TDD
Set up test infrastructure: fixtures, factories, test containers, page objects
Measure and report on coverage, mutation score, and test execution time
deliverables: Test strategy document (4-5 pages) with risk analysis, coverage targets, and test allocation
Unit test suite with at least 50 tests covering core business logic via TDD
Integration test suite with test containers for database and API interactions
E2E test suite with Playwright covering 5 critical user journeys using Page Object Model
Quality assessment report with coverage metrics, mutation testing results, and findings

criteria: Risk areas identified with severity and likelihood assessment
Testing effort allocated proportionally to risk (not equally across all features)
Coverage targets are justified by risk analysis, not arbitrary percentages
Strategy addresses all testing levels with clear boundaries between them
Unit tests cover core business logic with meaningful assertions
TDD evidence: git history shows test-first commits
Integration tests verify component interactions with real dependencies (test containers)
E2E tests cover critical user journeys with Page Object Model
All tests are independent, non-flaky, and run in under 10 minutes total
Fixtures provide reusable, isolated test setup
Factories generate realistic test data with sensible defaults
Test containers provide consistent database environments
Page objects encapsulate UI selectors and actions
Coverage metrics reported with analysis (not just numbers)
Mutation testing results identify gaps in the test suite
Findings are prioritized by risk and severity
Recommendations are specific and actionable
Comprehensive risk-based test strategy with justified targets
Multi-level automated test suite with TDD evidence
Well-designed test infrastructure (fixtures, factories, page objects)
Professional quality assessment with data-driven recommendations

major_assignments.title: CI/CD Pipeline with Performance and Security Testing
major_assignments.description: Build a complete CI/CD pipeline for the application tested in Assignment 1. The pipeline must include layered test execution, quality gates, performance testing with k6, security scanning with SAST and dependency checks, and deployment verification. Document the pipeline architecture and analyze the quality data it produces.
objectives: Configure a CI/CD pipeline with layered test execution and quality gates
Implement performance testing that detects regressions against baselines
Integrate SAST and dependency scanning into the pipeline
Set up deployment verification with smoke tests and monitoring
deliverables: CI/CD pipeline configuration (GitHub Actions YAML) with all stages
k6 load test scripts with baseline comparisons and performance analysis
Security scan configuration and vulnerability report with remediation plan
Pipeline architecture document (3-4 pages) explaining design decisions
Quality dashboard showing test results, coverage trends, and performance metrics

criteria: Pipeline has at least 4 stages with appropriate quality gates
Layered execution provides fast feedback (unit tests < 3 min)
Parallel execution configured where appropriate
Pipeline completes in under 20 minutes total
k6 scripts simulate realistic user behavior with multiple scenarios
Baselines established and regression detection configured
Results analyzed: p95/p99 latency, throughput, error rate reported
At least one bottleneck identified, fixed, and verified with re-test
SAST tool configured and running on every commit
Dependency vulnerability scanning configured
Findings categorized by severity with remediation plan
False positives documented and suppressed with justification
Pipeline architecture clearly documented with rationale for each decision
Quality dashboard shows meaningful trends over multiple pipeline runs
Performance analysis includes before/after optimization data
Security remediation plan prioritizes fixes by risk
Complete CI/CD pipeline with layered execution and quality gates
Effective performance testing with baseline regression detection
Security scanning integrated with actionable remediation
Clear documentation connecting pipeline design to quality goals

major_assignments.title: Quality Assessment and Process Improvement Report
major_assignments.description: Conduct a comprehensive quality assessment of the application and testing infrastructure built in Assignments 1 and 2. Analyze DORA metrics from the CI/CD pipeline, conduct an exploratory testing session, perform a testing maturity assessment, and produce a quality improvement roadmap with specific, prioritized recommendations.
objectives: Calculate and interpret DORA metrics from the project's CI/CD pipeline data
Conduct a structured exploratory testing session and document findings
Assess testing maturity against a standard framework
Create a prioritized quality improvement roadmap with measurable goals
deliverables: DORA metrics analysis with team performance classification and trend analysis
Exploratory testing session notes (at least 60 minutes) with bug reports and regression tests
Testing maturity assessment against the Testing Maturity Model
Quality improvement roadmap (5-6 pages) with prioritized actions, timelines, and success metrics

criteria: All four DORA metrics calculated correctly from pipeline data
Performance level classified with reference to industry benchmarks
Trends over the project duration analyzed and visualized
Bottlenecks in the delivery pipeline identified
Charter-based session with clear mission and scope
At least 3 defects discovered and documented with reproduction steps
Session notes follow SBTM format with timestamps and observations
Key findings converted to automated regression tests
Assessment covers all testing levels and practices
Current maturity level identified with evidence
Gaps between current and target maturity identified
Assessment is honest about weaknesses
Recommendations are specific, actionable, and prioritized by impact
Each recommendation has measurable success criteria
Timeline is realistic with dependencies identified
Roadmap connects improvements to DORA metric targets
Accurate DORA metrics analysis with actionable insights
Productive exploratory testing with documented findings
Honest maturity assessment with gap analysis
Prioritized improvement roadmap with measurable goals

major_assignments.description: Three major projects: test strategy/suite, CI/CD pipeline, and quality assessment

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is expected for lectures and mandatory for lab sessions. More than 3 unexcused absences may result in a reduced participation grade.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late assignments lose 10% per day. No submissions accepted more than 5 days late. Extensions granted only for documented emergencies.
course_policies.collaboration: Discussion of testing approaches and strategies is encouraged. All test code, reports, and analysis must be individual work unless explicitly designated as group work.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All submitted work must be your own. Copying test code from other students or external sources without attribution constitutes plagiarism. AI-generated test code must be reviewed, understood, and disclosed.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students need Python 3.12+ with pytest, Docker for test containers, a CI platform (GitHub Actions), Playwright for E2E testing, and k6 for load testing. Lab computers are available with all tools configured.
